content: A nested hierarchical memory gating mechanism derived from memory-efficient ICM can be repurposed to solve catastrophic unlearning of outdated behavioral priors in generalist open-world agents, where only 10% of high-activation long-term memory traces are retained for active adaptation while discarding redundant low-reward episodic traces. This creates a self-sustaining curiosity cycle where memory efficiency directly boosts the agent's ability to explore new environments without being constrained by the memory bloat of stale prior experiences.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
